evals.prompt: I need to add OAuth authentication to my Next.js app. It must support Google and GitHub as providers, protect admin routes, and store the session in a secure cookie.
evals.expected_output: The workflow activates with multi-agent planning: Planner decomposes into subtasks (OAuth config, providers, middleware, session), Implementer codes, Reviewer verifies security, Tester runs tests.
assertions: Uses the structured handoff protocol with TASK, SKILLS, ACCEPTANCE
The Reviewer verifies auth and cookie security
Security agent is triggered because auth is involved
The flow follows the order: Plan -> Implement -> Test -> Review -> Security

evals.prompt: The contact form doesn't send emails. Users report it stays loading forever after submit.
evals.expected_output: The workflow identifies this as a bug-fix, delegates investigation to the Planner for diagnosis, then Implementer fixes, Tester verifies the fix.
assertions: Uses the handoff protocol with relevant LESSONS_FILTER
The Implementer investigates the root cause before patching
The Tester verifies the form works post-fix
New lessons are recorded if corrections occur

evals.prompt: I want to understand how tasks are routed to different agents in this workflow.
evals.expected_output: The skill explains the task classification (quick, standard, full pipeline) and how model routing works via agent frontmatter.
assertions: Explains the three task paths: quick, standard, full pipeline
Mentions that model routing is automatic via agent frontmatter
References the skill system for progressive disclosure
